name: auth
description: Full authentication (better-auth): email/password, sessions, and — via config — OAuth and 2FA. Installs a global guard so the API is secure by default; health and /api/auth stay public.
env: # BETTER_AUTH_SECRET also derives the key that encrypts config secrets stored in the
# DB (OAuth client secrets, SMTP password). Keep it stable and secret; rotating it
# Everything below is OPTIONAL. OAuth providers, SMTP, and the server-enforced
# toggles (email verification, breached-password check, self-delete) — plus the
# feature flags — are configured on the admin Settings page, stored encrypted in
# the DB and applied live (no restart). These env vars still work as a fallback/
# override (e.g. for secrets-manager injection in production); the DB takes
# Email (Mailpit in local dev: docker compose --profile dev up → http://localhost:8025)
# Phone-number SMS webhook (dev: SMS sink on port 8095)
instructions: Set BETTER_AUTH_SECRET in .env (>= 32 chars). This also derives the key that encrypts config secrets stored in the DB, so keep it stable and secret.
Create the auth tables:  npx @better-auth/cli migrate -y --config apps/api/src/auth/auth.ts
Create the app tables (auth_config, app_setting):  npm run migration:run
Sign up:  curl -c cookies.txt -XPOST localhost:5002/api/auth/sign-up/email -H 'content-type: application/json' -d '{"email":"a@example.com","password":"password123","name":"A"}'
Call a protected route with the session:  curl -b cookies.txt localhost:5002/account/me

Everything is protected by default; mark public routes with @Public(). Configure OAuth (Google/GitHub), SMTP, and server-enforced toggles on the admin Settings page — stored encrypted in the DB and applied live (no restart). Environment variables (GOOGLE_CLIENT_*, SMTP_*, AUTH_*) still work as an optional fallback/override.
